title: Experience / Engineering Impact
introduction: Real-world engineering work — from cloud infrastructure migration to full-stack product delivery. Each entry focuses on technical decisions made and their measurable outcomes.

experiences.problem: The company's application stack ran on a single-region, manually managed AWS setup with no auto-scaling and unreliable deployments. The goal was to redesign the infrastructure for high availability and to eliminate manual release processes that were slowing down the engineering team.
decisions: Designed and deployed a Multi-AZ AWS 3-tier architecture with public/private subnets, NAT gateways, and Application Load Balancers for zero-downtime failover
Migrated from EC2-based deployments to ECS Fargate (serverless containers), reducing deployment time by ~40% and enabling automatic horizontal scaling
Containerized all services with Docker and standardized dev/prod environments to eliminate 'works on my machine' issues
Configured CloudWatch dashboards, log groups, alarms, and SNS notifications for full observability across all services
Automated the entire CI/CD delivery cycle with GitHub Actions — build, test, push to ECR, and deploy to ECS — eliminating all manual deployments
experiences.outcome: Deployment time dropped by approximately 40%. Infrastructure became fully self-healing under load with auto-scaling ECS tasks. Monitoring coverage went from zero to full observability within the first month. The team went from manual deployment every few days to automated continuous delivery.

experiences.problem: A business needed a unified web application to manage memberships, appointments, and access control — replacing a fragmented set of spreadsheets and manual processes. An additional requirement was adding biometric access via face recognition to replace physical badge scanning.
decisions: Built the full-stack application with Angular (frontend), Spring Boot (REST API), and MySQL — implementing membership lifecycle, appointment booking, and role-based dashboards for staff and admins
Implemented a secure face recognition module using Python and OpenCV, integrated as a microservice called by the Spring Boot backend during user authentication
Delivered iteratively under Agile/Scrum methodology with 2-week sprints, weekly demos, and structured backlog management
Designed database-level concurrency control for appointment booking to prevent double-booking under simultaneous requests
experiences.outcome: Delivered a production-ready application covering the full membership and appointment management lifecycle. The face recognition module achieved reliable authentication for enrolled users, replacing physical badge dependency. Project was validated as the PFE (Final Year Project) with high marks.

experiences.problem: Needed to deploy a full-stack application to a VPS with a custom domain, HTTPS, automated infrastructure provisioning, and a full DevSecOps pipeline — including security scanning at every stage, without relying on managed cloud services.
decisions: Containerized the full application stack with Docker Compose and managed service dependencies declaratively
Automated server provisioning and configuration with Ansible (Infrastructure as Code) — making the entire VPS setup reproducible from scratch in minutes
Built a CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions covering: build, automated tests, Trivy container scanning, Snyk dependency audit, SonarQube static analysis, and automatic deployment to VPS
Configured Nginx as a reverse proxy with Let's Encrypt SSL/HTTPS and strict firewall rules via UFW
Set up real-time monitoring with Prometheus & Grafana — tracking CPU, memory, request rates, and container health
experiences.outcome: Fully automated deployment pipeline from git push to live production. Security scanning integrated at every CI stage with no manual intervention. Full observability stack running on a self-managed VPS with zero managed services.
